services.ui_behavior: Generic error: '이런... Duck.ai를 일시적으로 사용할 수 없습니다. 잠시 후 다시 시도하세요.' with '다시 시도' link

response_headers.x-vqd-hash-1: MISSING — APF block response does NOT include this header

services.request_headers_note: Provisional headers shown — Accept: text/event-stream, Content-Type: application/json, Referer: https://duck.ai/

analysis.format_match: EventStream shows 3 events parsed correctly — rs_ JSON, msg_ JSON, [DONE]
analysis.root_cause: ERR_INVALID_ACTION persists despite correct event format. The DuckDuckGo Vercel AI SDK client validates 'actions' at app level beyond SSE event parsing.
analysis.missing_header: x-vqd-hash-1 header is ABSENT from APF block response. Real server (#427) returns ~2KB base64 JS challenge in this header. This may be a critical factor.
analysis.conclusion: SSE event format alone is insufficient. The client performs deeper validation — likely checking x-vqd-hash-1 header and/or action type fields within the parsed JSON that don't match expected Vercel AI SDK action types.

summary: FAILURE — rs_+msg_+[DONE] format from #427 RAW capture still triggers ERR_INVALID_ACTION. 3 EventStream events parsed correctly but app-level action validation fails. Key discovery: x-vqd-hash-1 response header is MISSING from APF response (real server returns ~2KB base64 challenge). Multiple format attempts (#419-#428) all fail with same error — DuckDuckGo client validation goes beyond SSE format to include header checks and/or Vercel AI SDK action type validation.
recommendation: Two possible approaches: (1) Include x-vqd-hash-1 header in APF block response — requires generating valid JS challenge code, (2) Investigate DuckDuckGo source code to understand exact 'action' validation logic that triggers ERR_INVALID_ACTION, (3) Consider block-only (no warning) strategy for DuckDuckGo if warning injection proves infeasible.
